title: Prioritizing Character Above Reputation | Sri Amitasana Dasa | SB 3.13.9 | 23.05.2024
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: ओम्नमो भगवते वासुदेवाया
ओम्नमो भगवते वासुदेवाया
ब्रह्मो वाचा प्रीतस्तुब्यमहं ताता
स्वस्तिस्ताद्वां शितिश्वरा
यन्मिर्व्यली के नहरदा
शादीनेत्यात्मनार्पितं
लोड ब्रह्मा सेड
माई डियर सन ओ लोड अफ ते वर्ड
आईं वेरी प्लीज़्ड विथ यू
आईं डिजाईर आल ब्लेसिंग्स फर बोत यू और वाइफ
You have without reservation surrendered yourself unto me with your heart for my instructions
The relationship between the father and the son is always sublime
The father is naturally disposed with good will towards the son
And he is always ready to help the son in his progressing life
But in spite of the father's good will, the son is sometimes misguided
Because of his misuse of personal independence
Every living entity, however small or big he may be, has a choice of independence
If the son is unreservedly willing to be guided by the father
The father is ten times more eager to instruct and guide him by all means
The father and son relationship as exhibited here in the dealings of Brahma and Manu is excellent
Both the father and the son are well qualified and their example should be followed by all humankind
Manu, the son, unreservedly asked the father, Brahma, to instruct him
And the father, who was full of Vedic wisdom, was very glad to instruct
The example of the father of mankind may be rigidly followed by mankind
And that will advance the cause of the relationship of fathers and sons
Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta Parpat
In the previous verse, we saw that how Manu,
Is requesting Brahma, who is his father
O worshipable one, please give me your direction for execution of duty
Within our working capacity
So that we can follow it for fame in this life and progress in the next
So very obediently, Manu is receiving instruction is one thing
Intense desire to execute the instructions is another thing
So here he is also saying
For the execution of duty within our working capacity
So that we can follow it
There are two components
One is receiving instruction
Prabhupada has given so many instructions
Prabhupada's spiritual master, Bhaktisiddhanta Saro, gave instruction to him
And he was very very eager to fulfill that instruction
So in this verse, in response to what Manu said, Brahmajai is saying
My dear son, O Lord of the world, I am very pleased with you
Now here, there is a conversation happening between Manu and Brahma
But actually, in the spiritual plane, in the spiritual life
This conversation is always going on between spiritual master and disciple
Between the superior and the subordinate

Relationship between the father and the son is always sublime.
The father is naturally disposed with good will towards the son, and he is always ready to help the son in his progress in life.
By default, father is always a well wisher of the son. He wants to bless his son, he wants to help his son.
But unfortunately, we also see in present times,
the son sometimes, because of misuse of his free will, is misguided and misuses his personal independence.
Every living entity, however big or small he may be, has a choice of independence.

शास्त्रा, scriptures, is the real father.
All shastras instruct that one should end his material way of life.
If one does not know the purpose of his father's order,
the shastras, he is ignorant.
A parent who does not know how to save his son
has no value because such father and mother
may be had in any form of life,
even among the cats, dogs and so on.
Only a father and mother who can elevate their son to the spiritual platform are bona fide parents.
One must understand how Maya works and how one can be released from the tossing waves of the river of innocence.
That is one's first duty.
So we have example of, even yesterday we had Vamana Jayanti.
We have examples of disobedience to superiors, but only if it is coming in the way of progress of Krishna consciousness, not otherwise.
We have examples of somebody even cheating. We have example of somebody even lying.
We have example of somebody breaking moral principles.
But they all are because they are serving the highest moral principles.
The gopis, in the middle of night, leaving their husband, leaving their family.
So we should be very clear, sometimes we can get stuck in morality.
No doubt. And we have to avoid extremes in here also.

We have to obey.
Income tax laws are there. We have to file returns.
I don't care.
So we have to respect the law of land.
But simultaneously know that, even in the laws, there are gradations.
And that maturity comes when it comes over a period of time.
Otherwise, sometimes we can be fanatics.
By and large, we follow the moral principles.
We don't tell lies.
We don't cheat.
We obey our elders.
And yes, we also have examples of higher principles.
And if situation arises, we can use those principles.
Now what is the general principle?
The thing is, our superiors, elders, spiritual master have to be respected.
When Grahaspati entered the assembly of the demigods,
they, this I have shared in previous earlier class also,
they and their king Indra took them for granted.
Since he came every day, they thought they did not have to show him special respect.
As it is said, familiarity breeds contempt.
Being very much displeased, Grahaspati immediately left Indra's palace.
Thus all the demigods headed by Indra became offenders at the lotus feet of Grahaspati.
And Lord Brahma, being aware of this, condemned their neglect.
We, when we are living in Vaishnava association,
or for that matter, somebody is serving Prabhupada very closely.
Even during Prabhupada's time, a lot of devotees got opportunity to do physical service.
They were personally serving Prabhupada.
Somebody was massaging his feet.
Somebody was travelling with him, helping him.
Somebody was helping with his daily requirements.
Secretary is assisting them.
So, on one side, it is a great opportunity to serve a spiritual master so closely.
The other side, there is a danger also.
Likewise, when we are living in association of devotees,
there is a great advantage.
We can benefit from devotees association.
We can get insulated from bad association.
Everywhere, when we are living in ashram, everywhere there is...
If you see outside hostel, it's all crazy pictures, crazy music.
People doing crazy things.
Our devotees go to IIT Bombay for preaching.
Regularly they go.
So, in the evening, most of the rooms, you know, you can see the smoke coming out.
Beer bottles lying outside.
And then sometimes devotees feel very embarrassed.
They open the room and all kinds of pictures are there.
Pictures are there.
Posters are there.
Some devotee was telling me in some IIT.
Not in Bombay, but some IIT.
The main director said,
You all are adults now.
We don't have to...
You are expected to behave maturely.
So, girls can come in the hostel anytime.
Boys can go anytime. Free.
No restriction. God will also not stop.
This is the western culture, which is not...
Which is going to really create havoc.
So...
So, we are living in association of devotees.
We are protected from all this.
But there is a danger also.

Familiarity breeds content.
They did not respect Brahaspati.
What happened as a result?

We can have a good reputation, we can be smart enough to show off, but Krishna, Prabhupada, they know what we are.
In fact, a devotee is humble because he knows I am so fallen.
We were talking about humility and gentleness.
He knows I don't have to cheat the world, I have so many flaws.
He genuinely believes, that's why there is no pride in him, there is no arrogance in him.
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu was asked, He said, I have no devotion for Krishna.
But he says, you are dancing, you are this and that, I am making a show.
See, how a devotee, he is such an advanced devotee, he is saying that is a show.
If I was actually advanced, I wouldn't be in this material world.
Without Krishna, I am living nicely.
This shows I have no devotion.
I have no love for Krishna.
These are true characters.
So, we should be true to Krishna.
We should be interested in, not reputation, but in character.
Sometimes in society, in community, we are more interested in reputation.
What will others think of me?
No doubt, as conditioned souls, we have that anartha, suddenly we cannot come out of it.
But at least theoretically, we should know.
Character is more important than reputation.
And we have to work hard, not to build the reputation, but we have to work hard to build the character.
If character is not good, reputation is good, what will happen?
In due course of time.
